what_it_does: Defines an authoring-layer contract over a fork's `resolve()` primitive, specifying how a coordinate (device, viewer, grain, and future axes) maps to resolved allocation slots for host/card layout and to resolvable prompt plus schema variables for roles, without introducing a new resolution engine.
observations: Reuses the fork's `resolve_slot` engine and only adds one optional prompt-template wrapper.
Coordinate axes are treated as registries; adding a new axis is a row change, not engine code.
Device axes include raw window dims (`device.w`, `device.h`), derived orientation, a derived/non-authoritative `device.kind`, and a pending `viewer.expertise`.
Slot grammar has continuous relationship-ASTs, discrete `select` lookups, literals, and an optional `{{name}}` prompt template.
Host allocation invariants (modal + shell) collapse three hand-enumerated layout modules into one invariant resolved on the same coordinate.
Card layout (`co-visible` vs `stepped`) is a resolved slot, not a stored variant, with `kind_panel` binding card kinds to panels.
Panel allocations are sourced from DNA `tokens.json` by token name, not hardcoded numbers.
Role `prompt_template` and `output_schema` become resolvable against the turn coordinate at run/read time.
`output_schema` is a Pydantic class, so grain is handled by read-side projection from a superset result or by extraction-side selection among predeclared classes.
§8 locks an adaptive forward-only stepped extraction: coarse output kind drives deeper extraction within the same pass, using one superset schema and a grain axis.
Fail-loud behavior is inherited from `resolve_slot`; degrade-clean fallback exists while `/api/resolve` is not live.
standouts: One invariant, never variants: host layout, card layout, and extraction schema each collapse to a single resolver-driven invariant.
Prompts and schemas are treated as resolver variables, with the schema-as-class wrinkle resolved by extract-once + read-side projection.
Adaptive forward-only extraction depth: the resolver reads its own coarse output to step deeper within the same pass.
conventions: Section-numbered contract with star-marked gates and pending decisions.
Frequent cross-references to tickets (t-...), commits (4207e75, 87fa59e), and files (runtime/resolver.py, roles.py, axes/resolution.py, dna/tokens.json).
Lane ownership and co-authorship model (composition, projection, fork, DNA, recollection).
Fail-loud, degrade-clean, and honest-flag language for non-authoritative or pending signals.
concerns: Many concrete values are pending other lanes (DNA token values, viewer.expertise source, exact kind-to-panel mapping), so the contract is forward-looking and may drift.
Dense terminology and heavy cross-referencing make the file hard to interpret without surrounding context.
`device.kind` is explicitly non-authoritative but still used as a derived discrete, creating a misuse risk.
The optional prompt-template wrapper is flagged as follow-up; its need and shape are not decided in this file.
notes: A dense cross-team contract that maps a generic resolver engine onto device-axis layout and role prompt/schema resolution, emphasizing one invariant and fail-loud resolution.
questions: What is the exact grammar and evaluator for the relationship-AST (e.g., `clamp`, `mul`, `field`)?
Which exact `card_kinds` map to panel A vs panel B in the verified two-panel structure?
How and when will `viewer.expertise` obtain its value?
What is the precise mechanism for registering a new axis in the axes-as-registries model?
How does the optional `{{name}}` prompt-template wrapper differ from the existing discrete `select` path in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file states it is an authoring contract over `resolve()` and indeed specifies coordinates, slot grammar, allocation/card invariants, and prompt/schema resolution.
apparent_themes: Reuse the engine, do not reimplement it
intention_signals: To unify layout and role prompt/schema decisions under a single resolver primitive, eliminate per-device or per-grain hardcoded modules, and lock a forward-only adaptive extraction model where depth is resolved from the data itself.
novelty: Treats prompt templates and Pydantic output schemas as resolver variables resolved against a coordinate, and handles the schema-class wrinkle by separating extraction-time class selection from read-time field projection over a single superset schema.
contribution: Provides a contract layer that connects a generic `resolve_slot` engine to concrete authoring concerns (device-axis layout, card panel allocation, role prompt/schema resolution) and defines the invariant/axis/registry vocabulary the fused system would keep.
summary_for_embedding: The RESOLVER CONTRACT is an authoring-layer specification over a fork's `resolve()` primitive, mapping coordinates such as device dimensions, orientation, viewer expertise, and resolution grain to resolved allocation slots for host/card layout and to resolvable prompt plus schema variables for roles.
intention_for_embedding: To replace hand-enumerated layout modules and static role prompt/schema declarations with a single fail-loud resolver-driven authoring system that supports adaptive extraction depth and read-time grain projection.
